role: OpPanels is the operation-panel region component that renders context-driven declarative panels alongside dynamically discovered, lazy-loaded extension panels, each isolated by an error boundary.
capabilities: Renders declarative panels from AppContext with PanelView
Passes current field value and setter into each panel
Lazy-loads each extension with React.lazy inside Suspense
Wraps every panel and extension in PanelErrorBoundary for contained failures
substance_why: It implements real rendering, context integration, lazy extension discovery, and per-item error isolation, not just a stub.
interactions_present: Panel field updates are wired by passing setField as onSet into PanelView
interactions_missing: No retry or user feedback when an extension fails to load
No controls for panel ordering, visibility, or collapsing
Extensions receive no shared value/onSet props
No explicit loading skeleton for panels themselves
performance_risks: import.meta.glob over ../extensions/*.tsx may generate many lazy chunks and increase build/runtime module graph size
No memoization of OpPanels or its children, so every context change re-renders all panels and Suspense boundaries
Each lazy extension fetches its chunk only at render time, causing potential waterfall requests
reuse_why: The declarative panel + lazy extension + error-boundary concepts are worth keeping, but the loader, rendering, and memoization should be rebuilt for the new architecture.
decomposition_hints: Split declarative PanelsRegion from lazy ExtensionsRegion
Extract extension discovery and registration into a dedicated hook/store
Extract the error-boundary-wrapped card layout into a reusable PanelSlot component
